original_text: The World Trade Organization eliminates tariffs on renewable energy technologies globally.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: The World Trade Organization issues a non-binding recommendation for member states to consider voluntarily reducing some tariffs on a narrow set of renewable energy technologies.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The World Trade Organization brokers a voluntary agreement among willing member states to partially reduce tariffs on major renewable energy technologies, with no formal enforcement mechanism.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The World Trade Organization establishes binding commitments to substantially reduce tariffs on renewable energy technologies globally, but with extended phase-in periods and broad exemption provisions.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The World Trade Organization eliminates tariffs on renewable energy technologies globally.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The World Trade Organization eliminates tariffs on renewable energy technologies globally, backed by robust compliance monitoring and an expedited dispute resolution process for violations.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The World Trade Organization eliminates tariffs and major non-tariff barriers on renewable energy technologies globally, enforced through rigorous compliance monitoring, expedited dispute resolution, and meaningful penalties for non-compliance.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The World Trade Organization eliminates all tariffs and non-tariff barriers on renewable energy technologies globally, with immediate enforcement, rigorous compliance monitoring, binding penalties for non-compliance, and funded technology-transfer programs to ensure equitable adoption across all member states.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier raises the comprehensiveness (scope of barriers addressed, breadth of coverage) and effectiveness (enforcement strength, compliance mechanisms, supporting programs) in a monotonically increasing manner, while all tiers remain grounded in the same core scenario of the WTO acting on tariffs for renewable energy technologies.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it maximizes both comprehensiveness (covering all tariff and non-tariff barriers) and effectiveness (immediate enforcement, binding penalties, and funded technology transfer), ensuring the policy achieves the fullest possible real-world impact on global renewable energy adoption.
